info.title: Ethos APIs - BannerErpAPIs
info.description: Native OpenAPI 3.1 conversion generated from the BannerErpAPIs Postman collection. Duplicate method/path variants were merged because OpenAPI cannot contain multiple operations with the same HTTP method and path.

post.summary: API Specifications 1.0.0 - Create a Custom API Definition
post.description:
### API Specifications 1.0.0 - Create a Custom API Definition

Creates a new custom API definition using a NIL GUID (`00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000`) and the resource name.
Generates the real GUID for the custom API and stores it in the GURASPC table.


Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerErpAPIs/api-specifications-1.0.0/api-specifications.yaml
x-postman-request-names: API Specifications 1.0.0 - Create a Custom API Definition

delete.summary: API Specifications 1.0.0 - Delete Custom API Definition
delete.description:
### API Specifications 1.0.0 - Delete Custom API Definition

Deletes a custom API using its GUID.
Removes the GURASPC definition and the GUID table associated with the API.


Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerErpAPIs/api-specifications-1.0.0/api-specifications.yaml
x-postman-request-names: API Specifications 1.0.0 - Delete Custom API Definition

put.summary: API Specifications 1.0.0 - Update an Existing Custom API
put.description:
### API Specifications 1.0.0 - Update an Existing Custom API

Updates an existing custom API definition.
Allows the following actions:
- Activate (`status: enabled`)
- Disable (`status: disabled`)
- Reset to test (`status: test`)
- Enable/disable OAuth (`oauthAllowed: true|false`)


Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerErpAPIs/api-specifications-1.0.0/api-specifications.yaml

---

### Endpoint Specifications 1.0.0 - Update Endpoint Specification for Custom API

Upload or update the Endpoint Specification for a custom API.
The endpoint specification: - Defines the URL path of the API   - Describes supported HTTP methods (GET, POST, etc.)   - Specifies parameters, responses, and headers   - Is stored in GURASPC_ENDPOINT_SPEC  
Media types for request and response: - Accept: application/vnd.hedtech.integration.endpoint-specifications.v1+json - Content-Type: application/vnd.hedtech.integration.endpoint-specifications.v1+json


Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerErpAPIs/endpoint-specifications-1.0.0/endpoint-specifications.yaml

---

### Logic Specifications 1.0.0 - Update Logic Specification for Custom API

Upload or update the Logic Specification for a custom API.

A successful PUT updates columns in GURASPC including:
- GURASPC_LOGIC_SPEC  
- GURASPC_RETURNED_MEDIA_TYPE  
- GURASPC_KNOWN_MEDIA_TYPES  
- GURASPC_MAJOR_VERSION  
- GURASPC_EVENT_DEF  
- GURASPC_METHODS  
- GURASPC_FILTERS  
- GURASPC_SECURITY_OBJECT  
- GURASPC_GUID_BASE_TABLE  
- GURASPC_SHADOW_TABLE  

GUID table and trigger generation occurs automatically for the resource.


Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerErpAPIs/logic-specifications-1.0.0/logic-specifications.yaml

---

### Resource Specifications 1.0.0 - Update Resource Specification for Custom API

Upload or update the Resource Specification (JSON Schema) for a custom API.
A successful PUT updates the following columns in GURASPC: - GURASPC_RESOURCE_SPEC   - GURASPC_RETURNED_MEDIA_TYPE   - GURASPC_KNOWN_MEDIA_TYPES   - GURASPC_MAJOR_VERSION  
The resource specification defines the JSON schema used for: - Documentation of the API   - Validation of incoming/outgoing data   - Establishing the API’s data contract  


Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerErpAPIs/resource-specifications-1.0.0/resource-specifications.yaml
x-postman-request-names: API Specifications 1.0.0 - Update an Existing Custom API
Endpoint Specifications 1.0.0 - Update Endpoint Specification for Custom API

post.summary: Applicant Institutional Awards 1.0.0 - Create a new award record in KVRAWRD table.
post.description:
### Applicant Institutional Awards 1.0.0 - Create a new award record in KVRAWRD table.

Creates a new Award Record. Once successfully created, the record will be visible on the KVAAWRD page.

The API accepts a single Applicant Institutional Award Record in the request payload, which will be persisted to the appropriate resource tables upon successful submission.

To create a discount award, set the discountAward parameter to 'Y'. Additional parameters are available for fee assessment, amount rounding, award locking, and discount award operations:

  • discountAward: Set to 'Y' to create discount-based award
  • feeAssess: Set to 'Y' to enable fee assessment
  • roundAmt: Set to 'Y' to enable amount rounding
  • lockAwrd: Set to 'Y' to lock the award after creation
  • acceptDiscAwrd: Set to 'Y' to automatically accept discount award
  • cancelDiscAwrd: Set to 'Y' to cancel discount award
  • declineDiscAwrd: Set to 'Y' to decline discount award
  • revokeDiscAwrd: Set to 'Y' to revoke discount award

All date fields must be provided in the DD-MM-YYYY format.

Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerErpAPIs/applicant-institutional-awards-1.0.0/applicant-institutional-awards.yaml

parameters.name: Accept
parameters.description: Version/media type accepted by this Banner/Ethos endpoint.

x-postman-request-names: Applicant Institutional Awards 1.0.0 - Create a new award record in KVRAWRD table.

post.summary: Applicant Institutional Awards 1.0.0 - Submit a batch of award records (both discount-based and non-discount based)
post.description:
### Applicant Institutional Awards 1.0.0 - Submit a batch of award records (both discount-based and non-discount based) to be posted to KVRAWRD.

Creates or Updates multiple Award Records in a single batch operation. Once successfully processed, the records will be visible on the KVAAWRD page.

The API accepts an array of Applicant Institutional Award Records in the request payload, which will be persisted to the appropriate resource tables upon successful submission.

For creating new records: Submit the record without an id field.

For updating existing records: Include the composite id structure containing studentId, aidyCode, aidpCode, fndcCode, and fndcSeqNo as shown in the example schema.

To create discount awards, set the discountAward parameter to 'Y' for each item. Additional parameters are available for fee assessment, amount rounding, award locking, and discount award operations:

  • discountAward: Set to 'Y' to create discount-based award
  • feeAssess: Set to 'Y' to enable fee assessment
  • roundAmt: Set to 'Y' to enable amount rounding
  • lockAwrd: Set to 'Y' to lock the award after creation
  • acceptDiscAwrd: Set to 'Y' to automatically accept discount award
  • cancelDiscAwrd: Set to 'Y' to cancel discount award
  • declineDiscAwrd: Set to 'Y' to decline discount award
  • revokeDiscAwrd: Set to 'Y' to revoke discount award

All date fields must be provided in the DD-MM-YYYY format.

Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerErpAPIs/applicant-institutional-awards-1.0.0/applicant-institutional-awards.yaml

parameters.name: Accept
parameters.description: Version/media type accepted by this Banner/Ethos endpoint.

x-postman-request-names: Applicant Institutional Awards 1.0.0 - Submit a batch of award records (both discount-based and non-discount based) to be posted to KVRAWRD.

post.description:
### Applicant Other Awards 1.0.0 - Submit a batch of applicant other awards to be posted to KVROTHR.

Creates or Updates multiple External Award Records in a single batch operation. Once successfully processed, the records will be visible on the KVAOTHR page.

The API accepts an array of External Award Records in the request payload, which will be persisted to the appropriate resource tables upon successful submission.

For creating new records: Submit the record without an id field.

For updating existing records: Include the composite id structure containing studentId, aidyCode, aidpCode, fndcCode, and fndcSeqNo as shown in the example schema.

/application-summaries?criteria={"applicationNumber":"1"}
```

```
/application-summaries?criteria={"applicationPreference":"1"}
```

Returns all admission applications for the admit code passed.
```
/application-summaries?criteria={"admitCode":"ST"}
```

Returns all admission applications for the Student Type Code passed.
```
/application-summaries?criteria={"studentTypeCode":"N"}
```
            
### Residency Code
Returns all admission applications for the residency code passed.
```
/application-summaries?criteria={"residencyCode":"R"}
```
            
### Application Status Code
Returns all admission applications for the Application Status Code passed.
```
/application-summaries?criteria={"applicationStatusCode":"D"}
```
                        
### Application Status Date
Returns all admission applications for the Application Status Date passed.
```
/application-summaries?criteria={"applicationStatusDate":"2013-09-17"}
```
Returns all admission applications greater than equals the Application Status Date passed.
```
/application-summaries?criteria={"applicationStatusDate":{"$gte": "2013-09-17"}}
```
            
Returns all admission applications greater than the Application Status Date passed.
```
/application-summaries?criteria={"applicationStatusDate":{"$gt": "2013-09-17"}}
```
            
Returns all admission applications less than equals to the Application Status Date passed.
```
/application-summaries?criteria={"applicationStatusDate":{"$lte": "2013-09-17"}}
```
            
Returns all admission applications less than the Application Status Date passed.
```
/application-summaries?criteria={"applicationStatusDate":{"$lt": "2013-09-17"}}
```

/application-summaries?criteria={"applicationDecisionCode":"25"}

get.summary: Ca Calgrant Roster 2.0.0-beta - Provide the list of ca calgrant roster.
get.description:
### Ca Calgrant Roster 2.0.0-beta - Provide the list of ca calgrant roster.

This API returns list of roster transaction records for a student Cal Grant award from CSAC (California Student Aid Commission).

Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerErpAPIs/ca-calgrant-roster-2.0.0/ca-calgrant-roster.yaml

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of resources requesting for this result set. The default limit is 500 and the maximum limit is 1000.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The 0 based index for a collection of resources for the page requested.

parameters.name: criteria
parameters.description:
### student filter

Returns all calgrant roster for the student ID passed.
```
/ca-calgrant-roster?criteria={"student":{"id":"4a0c95f7-0903-408a-8694-77b313809909"}}
```            
### aidyCode filter

Returns all calgrant roster for the aidy code ID passed.
```
/ca-calgrant-roster?criteria={"aidyCode":{"id":"3582835d-3e14-435b-b74f-4311746987ce"}}
```
### currentRecInd filter

Returns all calgrant roster for the current record indicator passed with value “Y”.
```
/ca-calgrant-roster?criteria={"calGrantData":{"currentRecInd":"Y"}}
```
### edLevelflag filter

Returns all calgrant roster for the education level flag passed with value “Y”.
```
/ca-calgrant-roster?criteria={"calGrantData":{"edLevelflag":"Y"}}
```            
### ssn filter

Returns all calgrant roster for the social security number passed with value “39801”.
```
/ca-calgrant-roster?criteria={"calGrantData":{"ssn":"39801"}}
```
### usdeCode filter

Returns all calgrant roster for the school code (include suffix) passed with value “04052021”.
```
/ca-calgrant-roster?criteria={"calGrantData":{"usdeCode":"04052021"}}
```            
### hsVerifyFlag filter

Returns all calgrant roster for the high school verify flag passed with value “Y”.
```
/ca-calgrant-roster?criteria={"calGrantData":{"hsVerifyFlag":"Y"}}
``` 

 ### Combination filters
 
 Note: The API supports combination filters on all the supported filter properties. Below are some of the examples:

 ### student and aidyCode
Returns all calgrant roster in combination with student ID and aidy code ID passed.
```
/ca-calgrant-roster?criteria={"student":{"id":"4a0c95f7-0903-408a-8694-77b313809909"},"aidyCode":{"id":"2b784c95-8856-4dce-8ec5-5b491297dc1b"},"calGrantData":{"currentRecInd":"Y"}}

 ```            

### student, aidyCode, currentRecInd, edLevelflag, ssn, usdeCode and hsVerifyFlag

Returns all calgrant roster in combination with student ID passed and aidy code ID and current record indicator with value “Y” and education level flag with value “Y” and social security number with value “39801” and school code (include suffix) with value “04052021” and high school verify flag with value “Y” passed.
```
/ca-calgrant-roster?criteria={"student":{"id":"4a0c95f7-0903-408a-8694-77b313809909"},"aidyCode":{"id":"2b784c95-8856-4dce-8ec5-5b491297dc1b"},"calGrantData":{"currentRecInd":"Y","edLevelflag":"Y","ssn":"39801","usdeCode":"04052021","hsVerifyFlag":"Y"}}
```            
### student, aidyCode, currentRecInd, edLevelflag, ssn, usdeCode, hsVerifyFlag and personFilter

Returns all calgrant roster in combination with student ID passed and aidy code ID and current record indicator with value “Y” and education level flag with value “Y” and social security number with value “39801” and school code (include suffix) with value “04052021” and high school verify flag with value “Y” and person filter ID passed.
```
/ca-calgrant-roster?criteria={"student":{"id":"4a0c95f7-0903-408a-8694-77b313809909"},"aidyCode":{"id":"2b784c95-8856-4dce-8ec5-5b491297dc1b"},"calGrantData":{"currentRecInd":"Y","edLevelflag":"Y","ssn":"39801","usdeCode":"04052021","hsVerifyFlag":"Y"}}&personFilter={"personFilter":{"id":"fd667408-7794-4b3f-add2-87e2d3bf0818"}}

parameters.name: personFilter
parameters.description:
Person Filter Id

Fetch all calgrant roster records for the person PIDMs identified through the person filter sent in the request. 
```/ca-calgrant-roster?personFilter={"personFilter":{"id":"6729a17e-3dfa-4517-95df-acdf160c24ab"}}```

parameters.name: Accept
parameters.description: Version/media type accepted by this Banner/Ethos endpoint.

post.summary: Catalog Course Additional Details Adjustments Process 1.0.0-beta - Creates or updates resources in SCRFEES, SCRATTR
post.description:
### Catalog Course Additional Details Adjustments Process 1.0.0-beta - Creates or updates resources in SCRFEES, SCRATTR, SCBSUPP, SCRSBGI, SCRCPRT, SCRCRDF, SCRINTG and SCRANGD.

A catalog course additional detail record will be created or updated with a POST request in one or all of the following - SCRGEES, SCRATTR, SCBSUPP, SCRCPRT, SCRCRDF, SCRINTG and SCRANGD. 


API behavior details

For catalog courses with no existing additional details, the POST request inserts course additional detail records as requested.

For existing catalog course additional detail records, the POST request replaces the effective term record with the course additional details included in the POST request. This means that the POST request  removes any existing data and replaces it with the data provided in the request.

post.summary: Catalog Course Bases Adjustments Process 1.0.0-beta - Update existing resources in SCBCRSE, SCRLEVL, SCRGMOD and SC
post.description:
### Catalog Course Bases Adjustments Process 1.0.0-beta - Update existing resources in SCBCRSE, SCRLEVL, SCRGMOD and SCRSCHD.


Updates the specified base catalog course record in SCBCRSE, along  with the associated level (SCRLEVL), grading mode (SCRGMOD), schedule details (SCRSCHD), or alias (SCRCALS) records.

API behavior details for courseDetail object:

* For updates to an existing effective term record, the POST request performs an "update" operation and updates only the fields provided in the courseDetail object. 
  Fields not included in the request will remain unchanged in the existing record.

* When a new effective term record is submitted, the maximum prior effective term record is copied and then the updates are applied.

API behavior details for levels, gradingModes and schedules arrays:

* Delete/Replace Functionality -  The POST request performs a "delete/replace" operation. When the POST request is processed, it will first delete any existing data  associated with the resource for the specified fromTerm (effective Term). After deletion, it will replace this data with the new data provided in the  request. This ensures that the resource is entirely updated to match the new data set provided.

* Complete Data Set Required - The POST request must include complete objects or arrays. This means that the data sent must fully represent  the resource being updated. Partial data may result in an incomplete or incorrect update.



  


Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerErpAPIs/catalog-course-bases-adjustments-process-1.0.0/catalog-course-bases-adjustments-process.yaml

parameters.name: Accept
parameters.description: Version/media type accepted by this Banner/Ethos endpoint.

get.summary: Check Payment Type Details 1.0.0 - Returns payment details for any kind of payment such as checks, direct deposits,
get.description:
### Check Payment Type Details 1.0.0 - Returns payment details for any kind of payment such as checks, direct deposits, and virtual cards.

Returns resources from FABCHKS.

Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerErpAPIs/check-payment-type-details-1.0.0/check-payment-type-details.yaml

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
The maximum number of resources requesting for this result set.
The default limit is 500 and the maximum limit is 1000.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The 0 based index for a collection of resources for the page requested.

parameters.name: criteria
parameters.description:
### Check Number 
Returns all payments based on check number.
```
/check-payment-type-details?criteria={"checkNumber":"Z0000001"}
```
### Check Date
Returns all payments based on check date.
```
/check-payment-type-details?criteria={"checkDate":"2023-10-31"}
```
### Origin
Returns all payments based on origin.
```
/check-payment-type-details?criteria={"dataOrigin":"Banner"}
```
### User ID
Returns all payments based on user identification.
```
/check-payment-type-details?criteria={"userId":"user"}
```
### Payment Type
Returns all payments based on payment type, with valid values Check, Virtual Card, and Direct Deposit.
```
/check-payment-type-details?criteria={"paymentType":"Direct Deposit"}
```

            
### Reconciliation Indicator
Returns all payments based on reconciliation indicator, with valid values Final, Regular, and None.
```
/check-payment-type-details?criteria={"reconciliationInd":"Final"}
```

            
### Activity Date
Returns all payments for the specified activity date.
```
/check-payment-type-details?criteria={"activityDate":"2023-11-05"}
```

            
### Check Type Indicator
Returns all payments having a specific check type indicator, with valid values Batch, Direct Deposit, Manual, Online, Zero Amount, Test, Void, and Rolled Back.
```
/check-payment-type-details?criteria={"checkTypeInd":"Direct Deposit"}
```

            
### Bank Code
Returns all payments by bank code.
```
/check-payment-type-details?criteria={"bankCode":"A1"}
```
 ### ACH Indicator

 Returns all payments made via direct deposit, with valid values true and false.

```
/check-payment-type-details?criteria={"checkAchInd": true}
```
            
### Check Amount
Returns all payments having a specific check amount.
```
/check-payment-type-details?criteria={"checkAmount":"600"}
```

            
### Check Cancel Reason Code
Returns all payments by cancel reason code.
```
/check-payment-type-details?criteria={"checkCancelReasonCode":"NLN"}
```

            
### Check Cancel Indicator
Returns all payments having a specific cancel indicator, with valid values true and false.
```
/check-payment-type-details?criteria={"checkCancelInd": true}
```

            
### Check Cancel Date
Returns all payments for the specified check cancel date. 
```
/check-payment-type-details?criteria={"checkCancelDate":"2023-10-31"}
```

parameters.name: Accept
parameters.description: Version/media type accepted by this Banner/Ethos endpoint.

parameters.description:
### Effective Term Code of Curriculum Rule
Returns all curriculum rules valid for the submitted term.
```
/curriculum-rules?criteria={"effectiveTermCode":"200810"}
```
### Curriculum Rule Number
Returns the curriculum rule for the submitted rule number.
```
/curriculum-rules?criteria={"curriculumRule":212}
```
### Program Code
Returns all curriculum rules where program code is equal to the submitted value.
```
/curriculum-rules?criteria={programCode":"BA-HISTORY"}
```
### Level Code
Returns all curriculum rules where the level code is equal to the submitted value.
```
/curriculum-rules?criteria={"levelCode:"UG"}
```
### Campus Code
Returns all curriculum rules where the campus code is equal to the submitted value.
```
/curriculum-rules?criteria={"campusCode:"M"}
```
### Initial Term Code of Curriculum Rule
Returns all curriculum rules where the inital term code is equal to the submitted value.
```
/curriculum-rules?criteria={"termCodeInitial":"200810"}
```
### College Code
Returns all curriculum rules where the college code is equal to the submitted value.
```
/curriculum-rules?criteria={"collegeCode":"AS"}
```
### Degree Code
Returns all curriculum rules where the degree code is equal to the submitted value.
```
/curriculum-rules?criteria={"degreeCode":"AAS"}
```
### Primary Curriculum Roll Indicator
Returns all curriculum rules where the primary curriculum roll indicator is equal to the submitted value.
```
/curriculum-rules?criteria={"primaryRollInd":true}
```
### Secondary Curriculum Roll Indicator
Returns all curriculum rules where the secondary curriculum roll indicator is equal to the submitted value.
```
/curriculum-rules?criteria={"secondaryRollInd":true}
```
### Lock Indicator
Returns all curriculum rules where the lock indicator equals the submitted value.
```
/curriculum-rules?criteria={"lockInd":false}
```
### Study Path Indicator
Returns all curriculum rules where the study path indicator equals the submitted value.
```
/curriculum-rules?criteria={"studyPathInd":true}
```

put.summary: Custom Table Configuration Revisions 1.0.0 - Update metadata attributes for an existing custom table.
put.description:
### Custom Table Configuration Revisions 1.0.0 - Update metadata attributes for an existing custom table.

This API allows updating supported metadata properties of a custom table without deleting or recreating it.

The request supports:

- Adding new non-key columns to the table.
- Updating supported metadata fields.

**Rules:**
- `attributeDataLength`: Can be increased, not reduced. For numeric columns in Oracle, length may be increased up to 38.
- `attributeDataScale`: Can be increased, not reduced.
- `attributeDisplay`: Can be updated only if the field is not required.
- `attributePrompt`: Can be updated.
- `attributeRequired`: Cannot be changed from `false` to `true`.
- `attributeDataType`: Cannot be changed.
- `columnName`: Cannot be changed.
- `columnBaseTable`: Cannot be changed.

After completion, the custom table API specification and OpenAPI definition are regenerated.

**Note:** Include only modified or newly added columns in the request payload. Unchanged columns do not need to be included.


Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerErpAPIs/custom-table-configuration-revisions-1.0.0/custom-table-configuration-revisions.yaml

---

### Custom Table Configuration Revisions 1.1.0 - Update metadata attributes for an existing custom table.

This API allows updating supported metadata properties of a custom table without deleting or recreating it.

The request supports:

- Adding new non-key columns to the table.
- Updating supported metadata fields.

**Rules:**
- `attributeDataLength`: Can be increased, not reduced. For numeric columns in Oracle, length may be increased up to 38.
- `attributeDataScale`: Can be increased, not reduced.
- `attributeDisplay`: Can be updated only if the field is not required.
- `attributePrompt`: Can be updated.
- `attributeRequired`: Cannot be changed from `false` to `true`.
- `attributeDataType`: Cannot be changed.
- `columnName`: Cannot be changed.
- `columnBaseTable`: Cannot be changed.

**New in 1.1.0:**
- `attributeUniqueColumn`: Indicates if column has a unique constraint.
- `attributeIndex`: Indicates if column is indexed.
- `attributeOrder`: Display order on UI.

After completion, the custom table API specification and OpenAPI definition are regenerated.

**Note:** Include only modified or newly added columns in the request payload. Unchanged columns do not need to be included.


Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerErpAPIs/custom-table-configuration-revisions-1.1.0/custom-table-configuration-revisions.yaml

parameters.description: Version/media type accepted by this Banner/Ethos endpoint.

Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerErpAPIs/custom-table-configurations-1.1.0/custom-table-configurations.yaml

---

### Custom Table Configurations 1.2.0 - Create a new custom table configuration in GORCSTT table.

The POST operation of the custom-table-configurations API is designed to support institutions in extending their database schema to meet unique business requirements by adding new custom table to the BAN_EXTENSIONS schema.


• Custom Table Configurations and Creating a Custom Table - The API allows administrators to define the schema of a custom table. It creates custom table configurations within the GORCSTT Banner configuration table and the system automatically creates a custom table in the BAN_EXTENSIONS schema dedicated to storing custom data specified by the administrators.

• Security Object and Specification based API - This operation facilitates the creation of a security object for the new custom table API and generates a spec-based API that supports standard CRUD operations (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE) with essential validations. The custom table's API definition is stored in the GURASPC Banner configuration table, and a comprehensive api-specification resource is created for interacting with the custom table.

• Custom Table and Custom Table API Naming Conventions - To ensure uniqueness and prevent future conflicts, Ellucian recommends prefixing the names of custom tables with 'x' and their corresponding APIs with 'x-'. For tables, this might look like 'xmaterial', and for APIs, 'x-xmaterial'. If not provided, the system generates API names by prefixing 'x-' followed by the custom table name.


• MEP (Multi-Entity Processing) Support - An optional MEP Indicator can be used to specify if the custom table should support multi-entity processing. Setting this indicator to 'Y' adds a VPDI_CODE column to the custom table for entity differentiation. Without this indicator or set to 'N', the custom table includes a tablename_vpdi_code column, with the MEP Indicator value always set to null.

• Enabling Auto-Increment - This API includes a feature that enables auto-increment for custom tables. Activating this feature by setting the "requiresAutoIncrement" property to true simplifies data management. It does so by adding a column named <TableName>_SEQUENCE_NUM to your custom table, which automatically generates unique identifiers for each record. This ensures each entry is uniquely identified and integrates this column into the primary key configuration of the table, thus enhancing data integrity. Please refer to "Auto-Increment feature in custom tables" in Banner Extensibility - Use for more information.

Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerErpAPIs/custom-table-configurations-1.2.0/custom-table-configurations.yaml

---

get.summary: Employee Deduction Details 1.0.0-beta - Get employee deductions
get.description:
### Employee Deduction Details 1.0.0-beta - Get employee deductions

Query and retrieve employee deductions data for a given criteria. Data is extracted from PDRBDED and PDRDEDN.

Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerErpAPIs/employee-deduction-details-1.0.0/employee-deduction-details.yaml

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of resources requesting for this result set.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The 0 based index for a collection of resources for the page requested.

parameters.name: criteria
parameters.description:
### Person ID
Returns the employee deduction information for this Person ID.  This filter should not be used if a Banner ID filter is used.
```
/employee-deduction-details?criteria={"personId": "085df706-ee15-4947-93f8-8c260e64795e"}
```
### Banner ID
Returns the employee deduction information for this Banner ID.   This filter should not be used if a Person ID filter is used.
```
/employee-deduction-details?criteria={bannerId":"123456789"}
```
### BenefitDeductionCode
Returns the employee deduction information for this Deduction.
```
/employee-deduction-details?criteria={"benefitDeductionCode":"LIF"}
```

 ### effectiveOn

Returns the employee deduction effective on this date.
```
/employee-deduction-details?criteria={"effectiveOn":"2024-01-09"}
```
### Status
Returns the employee deduction with this status. Valid values are:
  * `active`
  * `exempt` 
  * `terminated`
  * `waived` 

```
/employee-deduction-details?criteria={"status":"active"}
```

x-postman-request-names: Employee Deduction Details 1.0.0-beta - Get employee deductions

Returns all records for the specified Banner ID or temporary person Id.
```
/event-function-attendance-details?criteria={"registrantId":"A005417890"}
```

put.summary: Extension Configuration Revisions 1.0.0 - Update extension configuration
put.description:
### Extension Configuration Revisions 1.0.0 - Update extension configuration

Updates allowed metadata properties for an extended table. This includes:

- Adding new non-key columns to an existing extended table.
- Updates only the supported metadata attributes of the extended table.

  - `attributeDataLength`: Can be increased but not reduced. For numeric data types in Oracle, the length can be increased up to 38.  
  - `attributeDataScale`: Can be increased but not reduced.  
  - `attributeDisplay`: Can be updated only if the column is not required.  
  - `attributePrompt`: Can be updated.  
  - `attributeRequired`: Cannot be changed from `false` to `true`.  
  - `attributeDataType`: Cannot be changed.  
  - `columnName`: Cannot be changed.  
  - `columnBaseTable`: Cannot be changed.  

After updates, the extended table API specification and OpenAPI definition are regenerated.

**Note**: Include only modified or newly added columns in the request payload. Sending unchanged columns is not required.


Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerErpAPIs/extension-configuration-revisions-1.0.0/extension-configuration-revisions.yaml

---

### Extension Configuration Revisions 1.1.0 - Update extension configuration

Updates allowed metadata properties for an extended table. This includes:

- Adding new non-key columns to an existing extended table.
- Updates only the supported metadata attributes of the extended table.

  - `attributeDataLength`: Can be increased but not reduced. For numeric data types in Oracle, the length can be increased up to 38.  
  - `attributeDataScale`: Can be increased but not reduced.  
  - `attributeDisplay`: Can be updated only if the column is not required.  
  - `attributePrompt`: Can be updated.  
  - `attributeRequired`: Cannot be changed from `false` to `true`.  
  - `attributeDataType`: Cannot be changed.  
  - `columnName`: Cannot be changed.  
  - `columnBaseTable`: Cannot be changed.  

**New in 1.1.0:**
- `attributeUniqueColumn`: Indicates if column has a unique constraint.
- `attributeIndex`: Indicates if column is indexed.
- `attributeOrder`: Display order on the More Info page.

After updates, the extended table API specification and OpenAPI definition are regenerated.

**Note**: Include only modified or newly added columns in the request payload. Sending unchanged columns is not required.


Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerErpAPIs/extension-configuration-revisions-1.1.0/extension-configuration-revisions.yaml

### Extension Configurations 1.1.0 - Create a new resource

Create a new resource

Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerErpAPIs/extension-configurations-1.1.0/extension-configurations.yaml

---

### Extension Configurations 1.2.0 - Create a new extension configuration in GORRSDE table.

The POST operation of the extension-configurations API is designed to support institutions in extending their database schema to meet unique business requirements by adding new tables to the BAN_EXTENSIONS schema.


• Creating an Extended Table - The API allows administrators to define the schema of an extended table. Based on the configuration entries provided in the request, the system automatically creates this table within the BAN_EXTENSIONS schema.


• Configuration Entries - Upon creating an extended table, the system updates two Banner configuration tables GORRSDE which holds the new extended table definition and GURASPC which contains the new extended table API definition.


• Specification based API - Alongside the creation of the table, the system also generates an API specification for the newly created extended table. This specification includes operations such as GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE, complete with standard validations. This ensures that the extended table is readily accessible and manageable through API calls.


• Primary Key Identification - When defining the schema for an extended table, it is crucial for the administrator to identify and designate primary key columns. This information is necessary for the system to properly manage the extended table, especially in regard to maintaining data integrity and supporting efficient data retrieval operations.


• MEPed Tables - If the baseline (original) table is MEPed (Multi-Entity Processing enabled), the corresponding extended table will automatically inherit this characteristic. This ensures consistent handling of data across entities within the system.

• OAuth support for extended table APIs - When creating a new extended table API using the extension-configurations API, it is essential to ensure that the oauthAllowed flag is set to true. For more information, please refer to "OAuth Support for custom APIs" section in Banner Ethos API - Use.

• Extended Table Names - Ellucian recommends prefixing the names of extended tables with the letter 'x' (e.g., xnextofkin). This practice helps in avoiding naming conflicts with future Ellucian-delivered resources.

• API Names - Similarly, for extended table APIs, it is recommended to prefix the name with 'x-' followed by the extended table name (e.g., x-nextofkin). If a specific name isn't provided during configuration, the system generates one following this convention.


• When an extension configuration is created with the columns defined and if the property AtrributeRequired is set to true for a non-key column, then attributeDisplay must be set to true irrespective of the request having attributeDisplay as false or true. The user will not be informed and the response will have the property attributeDisplay set as true.

• If an extended column is defined such a way that column is a key column i.e columnBaseTable is specified, and if the request has attributeRequired set as N or Y, the response will have the attributeRequired property set to Y.

Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerErpAPIs/extension-configurations-1.2.0/extension-configurations.yaml

---

### Extension Configurations 1.3.0 - Removes the requested resource

Deletes the existing extension configuration record from GORRSDE table with the passed in identifier.
      

**NOTE**:
- Deleting an extension configuration requires the user to have access to the security object **API_EXT_CONFIGURATIONS_DELETE**.
- If the user has not created the configuration, then the user must have additional access to the security object **BAN_ETHOS_API_OVERRIDE** to delete configurations created by another user.

post.summary: External Payments 1.0.0 - Updated a record on GORCCAU and Create a new resource in TBRACCD
post.description:
### External Payments 1.0.0 - Updated a record on GORCCAU and Create a new resource in TBRACCD Reads a JSON file with one or more external payments, Searches in GORCCAU where gorccau_pay_trans_id equals transactionId and gorccau_status equals PENDING, updates this record with its new status. If the new status is SUCCESS, a new record is inserted in TBRACCD Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerErpAPIs/external-payments-1.0.0/external-payments.yaml --- ### External Payments 2.0.0 - Updates a resource in GORCCAU and Creates a new resource in TBRACCD

Searches resource in GORCCAU where GORCCAU_PAY_TRANS_ID = 'Provided TransactionId' and GORCCAU_STATUS = 'PENDING'.

Updates this resource with its new status and other received details.

If the new status is SUCCESS, a new resource will be inserted in TBRACCD.

Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerErpAPIs/external-payments-2.0.0/external-payments.yaml --- ### External Payments 3.0.0 - Creates or updates a payment record in GORCCAU. Conditionally creates resources in TBRACCD and TBRMEMO during updates.

Creates a new payment record in GORCCAU if the transactionId is sent as "0".

If a valid Banner transactionId is provided, it searches for a GORCCAU resource with matching transactionId and a status of 'PENDING' or 'IN_PROGRESS'. Then updates this resource with the new status and other received details.

If the provider status is 'SUCCESS', a new resource will be inserted in TBRACCD.

If the provider status is 'initiated' for a payment of type 'bank_transfer', a new memo will be recorded in TBRMEMO.

If the provider status is 'guaranteed' for a payment of type 'bank_transfer', the previously created memo will be deleted from TBRMEMO.

Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerErpAPIs/external-payments-3.0.0/external-payments.yaml --- ### External Payments 3.1.0 - Creates or updates a payment record in GORCCAU. Conditionally creates resources in TBRACCD and TBRMEMO during updates.

If the provider status is 'SUCCESS', a new resource will be inserted in TBRACCD.

get.summary: Faculty Advisors 1.0.0 - GET /api/faculty-advisors/{id}
get.description:
### Faculty Advisors 1.0.0 - GET /api/faculty-advisors/{id}


When the specified __term__ is not passed, the API recognizes the given person as faculty/advisor, when one of two conditions exists :

1.When the current date does not fall within any date range for any term and person has an active status for any term on the Faculty/Advisor Information Form (SIAINST)

 2.When the current date does fall within a date range and person has an active status for the term on SIAINST.
 
 When the specified term is passed, the API still uses the same logic, but restricts to the given term.


Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerErpAPIs/faculty-advisors-1.0.0/faculty-advisors.yaml

get.summary: Position Definitions 1.0.0-beta - Get position definitions
get.description:
### Position Definitions 1.0.0-beta - Get position definitions

Query and retrieve position definition data for a given criteria. Data is extracted from NBBPOSN.

Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerErpAPIs/position-definitions-1.0.0/position-definitions.yaml

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of resources requesting for this result set. The default limit is 500 and the maximum limit is 1000.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The 0 based index for a collection of resources for the page requested.

parameters.name: criteria
parameters.description:
### Position Code
Returns the position definition information for this Poisition Code.
```
/position-definitions?criteria={"positionCode": "A00001"}
```
### Position Title
Returns the position definition information for this Position Title.
```
/position-definitions?criteria={"positionTitle":"President"}
```
### Position Status
Returns the position definition information for this Position Status.
```
/position-definitions?criteria={"positionStatus":"active"}
```
### Position Class
Returns the position definition information for this position class.
```
/position-definitions?criteria={"positionClass":"ADM01"}
```
### Employee Class
Returns the position definition information for this employee class.
```
/position-definitions?criteria={"employeeClass":"01"}

```

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The 0 based index for a collection of resources for the page requested.

parameters.name: criteria
parameters.description:
### ID list  
Get holds filtering by ID list.
```
/proxy-holds?idList=HOPTE0204&idList=BANKA1
```
### from Date to Date 
Get holds between fromDate and toDate.
```
/proxy-holds?fromDate=2025-06-09&toDate=2025-06-10
```
### Hold Type Code List  
Get holds filtering by ID Hold Type Code List.
```
/proxy-holds?holdTypeCodeList=T1&holdTypeCodeList=T2
```
### Activity Date 
Get holds made between a range of dates.
```
/proxy-holds?startDate=2025-06-06&endDate=2025-06-06
```
### Originator Code List  
Get holds filtering by Originator Code List .
```
/proxy-holds?originatorCodeList=ADV1&originatorCodeList=ADV2
```
### Filters  
Get holds using multiple filters
```
/proxy-holds?id=HOSARDEMO&user=ECL001&holdTypeCode=T2&releaseInd=Y&originatorCode=ADV2
```

parameters.description: Version/media type accepted by this Banner/Ethos endpoint.

```
### User Id
Returns all purchase card transactions where the User Id is equal to the submitted value.
```
/purchase-card-transaction-details?criteria={"userId":"systest04"}
```
### Card Code
Returns all purchase card transactions where the card code is equal to the submitted value.
```
/purchase-card-transaction-details?criteria={"cardCode":"9999999999999998"}
```
### Feed Document Code
Returns all purchase card transactions where the feed document code is equal to the submitted value.
```
/purchase-card-transaction-details?criteria={"feedDocCode":"S00002A"}
```
### Bank Post Date
Returns all purchase card transactions where the bank post date is equal to the submitted value.
```
/purchase-card-transaction-details?criteria={"bankPostDate":"2023-07-27"}
```
### Bank Post Date Range
Returns all purchase card transactions where the bank post date falls between the submitted bank post start date and bank post end date.
```
/purchase-card-transaction-details?criteria={"bankPostStartDate": "2023-07-26","bankPostEndDate":"2023-07-28"}
```
### Surrogate Id
Returns all purchase card transactions where the Surrogate ID equals the submitted value.
```
/purchase-card-transaction-details?criteria={"id":674}
```

post.description:
### Registration Eligibility 1.0.0 - POST /api/registration-eligibility

The Request and Response of registration-eligibility API is similar to registration-register API except that there is an additional field called 'isEligibileStatus' in the response of registration-eligibility API. If 'isEligibileStatus' is true, it indicates that student is eligible to register for the specified term and crn.
  
When 'isEligibileStatus' is true, statusIndicator will be 'E' and statusDescription will be 'Eligible'.

post.summary: Registration Register 1.0.0 - POST /api/registration-register
post.description:
### Registration Register 1.0.0 - POST /api/registration-register

Running the API as an administrator persona has similar privileges as submitting the registration on SFAREGS.  The administrator is allowed to override the hold eligibility by submitting with the hold password.  The administrator is allowed to override the initial course registration status The default course registration status for an administrator is RE.  Submitting as an administrator requires adding the parameter for systemIn with a value of SB. 

The fields with the final registration status are in the __statusDescription__ and __statusIndicator__

  Status indicator of R means successful registration.

Status indicator of D means the class is dropped.

Status indicator of F means the failure, with failure message in statusDescription.

Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerErpAPIs/registration-register-1.0.0/registration-register.yaml

---

Submitting as an administrator requires adding the parameter for systemIn with a value of SB.

post.summary: Section Additional Details Adjustment Process 1.0.0-beta - Creates or updates resources in SSRFEES, SSRATTR, SSRSCC
post.description:
### Section Additional Details Adjustment Process 1.0.0-beta - Creates or updates resources in SSRFEES, SSRATTR, SSRSCCD, SSRANGD, SSRCLBD and SORFNCR.


A section additional details record will be created or updated with a POST request in one or all of the following -  SSRFEES, SSRATTR, SSRSCCD, SSRANGD, SSRCLBD and SORFNCR.


API behavior details:

* For sections with no existing additional details, the POST request inserts section additional detail records as requested.

* For existing section additional detail records, the POST request replaces any existing data associated  with the resource for the specified term and crn with the new data provided in the request. This ensures that the  resource is entirely updated to match the new data set provided.

* Complete Data Set Required - The POST request must include complete objects or arrays. This means that the data sent must fully represent  the resource being updated. Partial data may result in an incomplete or incorrect update.


Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerErpAPIs/section-additional-details-adjustment-process-1.0.0/section-additional-details-adjustment-process.yaml

post.description:
### Section Bases Submission Process 1.0.0-beta - Creates or updates resources in SSBSECT, SSRRESV, SSRSPRT, SSRSRDF, SSRRSTS, SSREXTN or SSRRFND.



Creates a base section record or updates an existing base section record in SSBSECT, along  with the associated section partition (SSRSPRT) and section room attribute (SSRSRDF) preference records.  For Open Learning  Registration sections, the registration status codes (SSRRSTS) and associated extension processing (SSREXTN) and refunding  rules (SSRRFND) may also be updated.

API behavior details

* Replace Functionality -  When the POST request is processed, it will replace any existing data associated  with the resource for the specified term and crn with the new data provided in the request. This ensures that the  resource is entirely updated to match the new data set provided.

* Complete Data Set Required - The POST request must include complete objects or arrays. This means that the data sent must fully represent  the resource being updated. Partial data may result in an incomplete or incorrect update.

post.summary: Section Additional Details Adjustment Process 1.0.0-beta - Submit a batch of section additional details data to SSR
post.description:
### Section Additional Details Adjustment Process 1.0.0-beta - Submit a batch of section additional details data to SSRFEES, SSRATTR, SSRSCCD, SSRANGD, SSRCLBD and SORFNCR.

A batch of section additional details records will be created or updated with a POST request
 in one or all of the following - SSRFEES, SSRATTR, SSRSCCD, SSRANGD, SSRCLBD and SORFNCR.


API behavior details:

* For sections with no existing additional details, the POST request inserts section additional detail records as requested.

* For existing section additional detail records, the POST request replaces any existing data associated  with the resource for the specified term and crn with the new data provided in the request. This ensures that the  resource is entirely updated to match the new data set provided.

* Complete Data Set Required - The POST request must include complete objects or arrays. This means that the data sent must fully represent  the resource being updated. Partial data may result in an incomplete or incorrect update.


Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerErpAPIs/section-additional-details-adjustment-process-1.0.0/section-additional-details-adjustment-process.yaml

---

### Section Descriptors Adjustment Process 1.0.0-beta - Submit a batch of section data to (SSRSYLN, SSRSYLO, SSRSYRM, SSRSYTR), section description (SSBDESC), and course text (SSRTEXT).

A batch of section descriptor records will be created or updated with a POST request in one or all of the following: SSRSYLN,  SSRSYLO, SSRSYRM, SSRSYTR, SSBDESC, and SSRTEXT.

API behavior details:

* Replace Functionality - When the POST request is processed, it will replace any existing data associated with the  resource for the specified term and section with the new data provided in the request. This ensures the record is entirely updated to match the new data set provided.

* Complete Data Set Required - The POST request must include complete objects or arrays. The data sent must fully reporesent the resource being updated.  Partial data may result in an incomplete or incorrect udpate. 
* For existing section descriptor records, the POST request replaces the effective record with the section descriptor included  in the POST request. This means that the POST request removes any existing data and replaces it with the data provided in the request.

post.summary: Section Requisites and Connections Adjustment Process 1.0.0-beta - Creates or updates resources in SSBSECT, SSRLINK
post.description:
### Section Requisites and Connections Adjustment Process 1.0.0-beta - Creates or updates resources in SSBSECT, SSRLINK, SSRCORQ and SSRRTST.

Creates or updates the specified section link (SSRLINK), section corequisite (SSRCORQ),  and section test score and prerequisite restrictions tab (SSRRTST).

API behavior details:

* For sections with no existing requisites and connections, the POST requests inserts section requisite and connection records as requested.

* For existing section requisite and connection records, the POST request replaces any existing data associated with the  resource for the specified term and crn with the new data provided in the request. This ensures the record is entirely updated to match the new data set provided.

* Complete Data Set Required - The POST request must include complete objects or arrays. The data sent must fully represent the resource being updated.  Partial data may result in an incomplete or incorrect update.

get.description:

Returns all term codes associated with  SSBSECT, SOBPTRM, SCBCRSE, SIRASGN, SSRMEET, SPRIDEN, SPBPERS, GOREMAL, GOBPTAC and STVSSTS are returned.

Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerErpAPIs/section-schedule-details-1.0.0/section-schedule-details.yaml

---

### Section Schedule Details 1.1.0 - A bulk extract of Section Schedule Details associated with a Term Code.

Returns all term codes associated with  SSBSECT, SOBPTRM, SCBCRSE, SIRASGN, SSRMEET, SPRIDEN, SPBPERS, GOREMAL, GOBPTAC and STVSSTS are returned.

Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerErpAPIs/section-schedule-details-1.1.0/section-schedule-details.yaml

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
The maximum number of resources requesting for this result set.\
The default limit is 500 and the maximum limit is 1000.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The 0 based index for a collection of resources for the page requested.

parameters.name: criteria
parameters.description:
### Term Code
Returns all records for the termCode.
```
/section-schedule-details?criteria={"termCode":"201410"}
```
### Course Reference Number   
Returns all records for the crn.
```
/section-schedule-details?criteria={"crn":"10006"}
```
### Subject Code   
Returns all records for the subjectCode.
```
/section-schedule-details?criteria={"subjectCode":"ACCT"}
```
### Course Number   
Returns all records for the courseNumber.
```
/section-schedule-details?criteria={"courseNumber":"2310"}
```
### Status Code   
Returns all records for the statusCode.
```
/section-schedule-details?criteria={"statusCode":"P"}
```
### Status Active Indicator   
Returns all records for the statusActiveInd.
```
/section-schedule-details?criteria={"statusActiveInd":"A"}
```
### Part Of term   
Returns all records for the partOfTerm.
```
/section-schedule-details?criteria={"partOfTerm":"1"}
```
### Campus   
Returns all records for the campus.
```
/section-schedule-details?criteria={"campus":"M"}
```
### Course College Code   
Returns all records for the courseCollegeCode.
```
/section-schedule-details?criteria={"courseCollegeCode":"BU"}
```
### Email Code   
Returns all records for the emailCode.
```
/section-schedule-details?criteria={"instructors": [{"instructorEmails": [{"emailCode": "PTST"}]}]}
```
### Email Preferred Indicator   
Returns all records for the emailPreferredInd.
```
/section-schedule-details?criteria={"instructors": [{"instructorEmails": [{"emailPreferredInd": true}]}]}
```
### Section Attribute Code  
Returns all records for the sectionAttributeCode.
```
/section-schedule-details?criteria={"sectionAttributes": [{"sectionAttributeCode": "AFOU"}]}
```
### Alternate ID Name type  
Returns all records for the alternateIdNameType.
```
/section-schedule-details?criteria={ "instructors": [{"instructorAlternateIdentifications": [{"alternateIdNameType": "CASE"}]}]}
```
 ### OR Operators

 It is supported for crn, subjectCode, courseNumber, statusCode, campus, courseCollegeCode, sectionAttributeCode and alternateIdNameType. An OR query can be performed using the $or operator. The example below will return all instances where subjectCode equals ACCT or subjectCode equals BIOL.

```
/section-schedule-details?criteria={"$or":[{"subjectCode": "ACCT"},{"subjectCode": "BIOL"}]}
```

parameters.description: Version/media type accepted by this Banner/Ethos endpoint.

get.summary: Student Learner Curriculum Status Details 1.0.0 - Returns current and active learner curriculum records from SORLCU
get.description:
### Student Learner Curriculum Status Details 1.0.0 - Returns current and active learner curriculum records from SORLCUR

Returns all current and active SORLCUR records for the learner module (SORLCUR_LMOD_CODE = 'LEARNER') and selected curriculum status details.

Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerErpAPIs/student-learner-curriculum-status-details-1.0.0/student-learner-curriculum-status-details.yaml

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
The maximum number of resources requesting for this result set.\
The default limit is 500 and the maximum limit is 1000.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The 0 based index for a collection of resources for the page requested.

parameters.name: criteria
parameters.description:
### Banner Id (required)
Returns all learner curriculum records for the Banner ID passed.
```
/student-learner-curriculum-status-details?criteria={"bannerId":"331314209"}
```
### Effective Term (required)
Returns all learner curriculum records in effect for the term passed.
```
/student-learner-curriculum-status-details?criteria={"effectiveTerm":"201010"}
```
### Graduation Application Number
Returns all learner curriculum records tagged with the graduation application number passed.
```
/student-learner-curriculum-status-details?criteria={"graduationApplicationNumber":3}
```
### Priority Number
Returns the learner curriculum record for the priority number passed.
```
/student-learner-curriculum-status-details?criteria={"priorityNumber":1}
```

parameters.name: Accept
parameters.description: Version/media type accepted by this Banner/Ethos endpoint.

update the status of student in SGBSTDN (SGBSTDN_STYP_CODE). The following status are recognized:  a) First Time Student in College -> SGBSTDN_STYP_CODE = 1; b) Concurrent Student  -> SGBSTDN_STYP_CODE = Y; c) First-Time Transfer -> SGBSTDN_STYP_CODE = 2; d) Returning Student   -> SGBSTDN_STYP_CODE = 3; e) Continuing Student  -> SGBSTDN_STYP_CODE = 5.

  If term is passed, the fields “totalCue”, totalCredit” and “totalBill” retrieves appropriate values else they always return “0”.

parameters.name: criteria
parameters.description:
### Term Code (Required) 

Returns all records for the termCode passed. 
```
/term-enrollments?criteria={"termCode":"202122"} 
```            
### Enrollment Status Code 

Returns all records for the enrollmentStatusCode passed. 
```
/term-enrollments?criteria={"termCode":"202122", "enrollmentStatusCode":"EL"} 
```
### Student Id

Returns all records for the studentId passed. 
```
/term-enrollments?criteria={"termCode":"202122", "studentId":"331314209"} 
```
### Course Registration Status Code 

Returns all records for the courseRegistrationStatusCode passed. 
```
/term-enrollments?criteria={"termCode":"202122", "sectionRegistrations": {"$or": [{"courseRegistrationStatusCode": "RW"}, {"courseRegistrationStatusCode": "EL"}]}}
```            
### Course Reference Number 

Returns all records for the courseReferenceNumber passed. 
```
/term-enrollments?criteria={"termCode":"202122", "sectionRegistrations": {"$or": [{"courseReferenceNumber": "2007"}, {"courseReferenceNumber": "10012"}]}} 
```
### Course Number 

Returns all records for the courseNumber passed. 
```
/term-enrollments?criteria={"termCode":"202122", "sectionRegistrations": {"$or": [{"courseNumber": "1401"},  {"courseNumber": "2010"}]}}
```            
### Subject Code 

Returns all records for the subjectCode passed. 
```
/term-enrollments?criteria={"termCode":"202122", "sectionRegistrations": {"$or": [{"subjectCode": "BIOL"}, {"subjectCode": "ANTH"}]}}
```            
### Part of Term Code 

Returns all records for the partOfTermCode passed. 
```
/term-enrollments?criteria={"termCode":"202122", "sectionRegistrations": {"$or": [{"partOfTermCode": "1"}, {"partOfTermCode": "2"}]}}
```
### Section Sequence Number 

Returns all records for the sectionSequenceNumber passed. 
```
/term-enrollments?criteria={"termCode":"202122", "sectionRegistrations": {"$or": [{"sectionSequenceNumber": "2"}, {"sectionSequenceNumber": "001"}]}}
```            
### Email Code 

Returns all records for the emailCode passed. 
```
/term-enrollments?criteria={"termCode":"202122", "studentDetails": {"emails": {"emailCode": "0001"}}}
```            
### Email Preferred Indicator

Returns all records for the emailPreferredInd passed. 
```
/term-enrollments?criteria={"termCode":"202122", "studentDetails": {"emails": {"emailPreferredInd": true}}} 
```
### Telephone Code 

Returns all records for the telephoneCode passed. 
```
/term-enrollments?criteria={"termCode":"202122", "studentDetails" : {"telephones" : {"$or" : [{"telephoneCode": "MA"}, {"telephoneCode": "PR"}]}}}
```            
### Address Type 

Returns all records for the addressType passed. 
```
/term-enrollments?criteria={"termCode":"202122", "studentDetails" : {"addresses" : {"$or" : [{"addressType": "BB"}, {"addressType": "HQ"}]}}}
```            
### Program 

Returns all records for the program passed. 
```
/term-enrollments?criteria={"termCode":"202122", "curricula" : {"$or" : [{"program": "BA-ANTHRO"}, {"program" :  "BSCE"}]}}
```
### Level Code 

Returns all records for the levelCode passed. 
```
/term-enrollments?criteria={"termCode":"202122", "curricula" : {"$or" : [{"levelCode" :  "CR"}, {"levelCode" : "US"}]}}
```
### Field of Study Type 

Returns all records for the fieldOfStudyType passed. 
```
/term-enrollments?criteria={"termCode":"202122", "curricula" : {"fieldsOfStudy" : {"$or": [{"fieldOfStudyType":"MAJOR"}, {"fieldOfStudyType":"MINOR"}]}}}
```

get.description:
### Vendor Details 1.0.0 - Returns vendor details for all vendors, or using one of 18 parameter options returns vendor details for selected vendors.  (The number of vendors returned in a payload is regulated by the resource limit, a maximum of 500 vendors at a time.)


Returns resources from FTVVEND, FTVVENT, FTRVTCL, SPRIDEN, SPBPERS, SPRADDR, SPRTELE, and GOREMAL.


Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerErpAPIs/vendor-details-1.0.0/vendor-details.yaml

---

### Vendor Details 2.0.0 - Returns vendor details for all vendors, or using one of 18 parameter options returns vendor details for selected vendors.  (The number of vendors returned in a payload is regulated by the resource limit, a maximum of 500 vendors at a time.)


Returns resources from FTVVEND, FTVVENT, FTRVTCL, SPRIDEN, SPBPERS, SPRADDR, SPRTELE, and GOREMAL.


Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerErpAPIs/vendor-details-2.0.0/vendor-details.yaml

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
The maximum number of resources requesting for this result set.
The default limit is 500 and the maximum limit is 500.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The 0 based index for a collection of resources for the page requested.

parameters.name: criteria
parameters.description:
### Vendor ID
Returns vendor details for the given Vendor ID (guid).
```
/vendor-details/criteria={"id":"a8d212c6-2f18-495c-9af0-a0d4e9ab8422"}
```
### vendorCode
Returns vendor details for the given Vendor Code.
```
/vendor-details?criteria={"vendorCode":"TESTVEND"}
```
### personIndicator
Returns vendor details based on the type of person. Person indicator values are "Person" and "Organization".
```
/vendor-details?criteria={"vendorDetails":{"personIndicator": "Person"}}
```
### lastNamePrefix
Returns vendor details based on last name prefix.
```
/vendor-details?criteria={"vendorDetails":{"lastNamePrefix":"LN"}}
```
### firstName
Returns vendor details based on first name.
```
/vendor-details?criteria={"vendorDetails":{"firstName": "VIFIRST"}}
```
### lastName
Returns vendor details based on last name.
```
/vendor-details?criteria={"vendorDetails":{"lastName": "VILAST"}}
```
### middleName
Returns vendor details based on middle name.
```
/vendor-details?criteria={"vendorDetails":{"middleName": "VIMID"}}
```
### firstName, middleName, lastName
Returns vendor details based on first name, middle name, and last name.
```
/vendor-details?criteria={"vendorDetails":{"firstName": "VIFIRST","middleName": "VIMID","lastName": "VILAST"}}
```
### vendorDetails id
Returns vendor details based on the Person ID (guid) or Organization ID (guid).
```
/vendor-details?criteria={"vendorDetails":{"id":"f1f9a3da-8faf-4027-9094-d31b2712c1ad"}}
```
### eProcurementIndicator
Returns vendor details based on the eProcurement indicator. Values are "Yes" and "No".
```
/vendor-details?criteria={"eProcurementIndicator": "No"}
```
### taxFormStatus
Returns vendor details for the given Tax Form Status.
```
/vendor-details?criteria={"taxFormStatus": "91"}
```
### vendorClassifications Code
Returns vendor details for the given Vendor Type Classification Code.
```
/vendor-details?criteria={"vendorClassifications":{"code": ["OS","IS"]}}
```
### vendorClassifications ID
Returns vendor details for the given Vendor Type Classification ID (guid).
```
/vendor-details?criteria={"vendorClassifications":{"id":"591ed257-4a6c-48d1-9031-a21851572a51"}}
```
### vendorCode and vendorClassifications Code
Returns vendor details for the given Vendor Code and Vendor Type Classification Code.
```
/vendor-details?criteria={"vendorCode":"VITEST","vendorClassifications":{"code": "OS"}}
```
### Vendor ID and vendorClassifications Code
Returns vendor details for the given Vendor ID (guid) and Vendor Type Classification Code.
```
/vendor-details?criteria={"id":"a8d212c6-2f18-495c-9af0-a0d4e9ab8422","vendorClassifications":{"code": "OS"}}
```
### Address ID
Returns vendor details for the given Address ID (guid).
```
/vendor-details?criteria={"addresses":{"id": "102c56a6-e351-414e-bac6-3a31685969fc"}}
```
### Vendor ID, addressType Code, and addressSequence Number
Returns vendor details for the given Vendor ID (guid), Address Type, and Address Sequence.
```

post.summary: Vendor Details 1.0.0 - Creates or updates vendor details, for one vendor at a time.
post.description:
### Vendor Details 1.0.0 - Creates or updates vendor details, for one vendor at a time.

This API can be used to create and update vendor information for Banner Finance in the following tables:
- FTVVEND, FTVVENT, FTRVTCL, SPRIDEN, SPBPERS, SPRADDR, SPRTELE, GOREMAL

From a single payload the POST API can update all vendor related tables including Vendor Name, Vendor Address, Vendor Telephone, Vendor Email, Vendor Type, and Taxes Collected related to the Vendor Maintenance (FTMVEND) page.

From the same payload, the POST method supports Insert and Update for vendor values.

- For example with an existing vendor, the API can add new property values previously null and at the same time overwrite or remove existing property values.

- For example with a new vendor, the API can be used to update a Person ID and add new payment addresses.

With the POST method updates, existing property values will not be removed unless sent as a null value property, for example:

- To change Address from three lines to two lines, send Address Line 3 with a null value.

- To change Telephone with Extension to Telephone without Extension, send the new Telephone and Telephone Extension with a null value.

The POST method does not support Delete for Address, Telephone, Email, Vendor Type, and Taxes Collected.

- Taxes Collected can be removed using this API by resetting a vendor to Collects No Taxes and then reverting to Collects Selected Taxes adding back the desired codes for Taxes Collected.

Deletions for Address and Email can use different APIs from the Ethos Data Model set of General Person APIs.


Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerErpAPIs/vendor-details-1.0.0/vendor-details.yaml

---

### Vendor Details 2.0.0 - Creates or updates vendor details, for one vendor at a time.

This API can be used to create and update vendor information for Banner Finance in the following tables:
- FTVVEND, FTVVENT, FTRVTCL, SPRIDEN, SPBPERS, SPRADDR, SPRTELE, GOREMAL

From a single payload the POST API can update all vendor related tables including Vendor Name, Vendor Address, Vendor Telephone, Vendor Email, Vendor Type, and Taxes Collected related to the Vendor Maintenance (FTMVEND) page.

From the same payload, the POST method supports Insert and Update for vendor values.

- For example with an existing vendor, the API can add new property values previously null and at the same time overwrite or remove existing property values.

- For example with a new vendor, the API can be used to update a Person ID and add new payment addresses.

With the POST method updates, existing property values will not be removed unless sent as a null value property, for example:

- To change Address from three lines to two lines, send Address Line 3 with a null value.

- To change Telephone with Extension to Telephone without Extension, send the new Telephone and Telephone Extension with a null value.
The POST method supports Delete for Email, Vendor Type, and Taxes Collected.
The POST method does not support Delete for Address, and Telephone.
        
Deletions for Address can use different APIs from the Ethos Data Model set of General Person APIs.


Source OpenAPI file: ethosapis/BannerErpAPIs/vendor-details-2.0.0/vendor-details.yaml
